contents:
Measurements will be added next time these are made. Here’s a quick summary for now.
Ingredients Wet okara (cooked okara works well, but raw is fine too since these get cooked a lot — if your okara is dry, add any liquid like plant milk, broth, tomato product, etc.) Vital wheat gluten (VWG) Flour Chick’n bouillon (or any bouillon, or whatever spices you want) Instructions Make the dough Use a food processor to blend for 30 seconds or until well combined. The dough should clump up and come away from the walls of the food processor, like seitan.
Let it autolyse in the food processor for anywhere from 15 minutes to several hours.
After that, you can optionally blend in some dense or frozen tofu, beans, or veggies. Don’t blend too much — these can be chunky.
First bake Squish the dough into nugget shapes and lay them on a baking sheet. Bake for 15 minutes each side at 380°F. After this they’re edible but squishy.
Second cook (pick one) Marinade + air fry — make a marinade and air fry until crispy Pan fry — cut them up and pan fry Panko crust + bake — make a wet dredge with plant milk and flax (optionally with butter or oil) and a dry dredge with panko, starch or flour, and any spices. Wet dredge then dry dredge the nuggets, optionally spray with oil (without oil the panko will stay white and may burn quickly, so keep an eye on them). Bake at 400°F or broil until golden on each side. Notes Loosely based on this recipe by Chez Jorge if you need measurements.
title: Okara Nuggets

contents:
Ingredients 2 cups dry brown lentils 1.7-ish liters water (for boiling) 2 tbsp white vinegar 2.5 tsp tempeh starter (Raprima bulk starter — store in the fridge) Equipment 2 sandwich bags Toothpick (for poking holes in bags) Small kitchen scale Oven with a functioning light (acts as incubator) Large mixing bowl Kitchen towel (for cooling lentils) Strainer Racks Thermometer Instructions Step 1 — Cook the lentils Rinse and cook lentils for 12-13 minutes but NO MORE. Meanwhile poke lots of holes in the sandwich bags w the toothpick.
Step 2 — Cool the lentils Drain and let cool preferably spread out on a kitchen towel laid over a large rack. Come back in 30min or so
Step 3 — Mix in starter Put lentils in a large mixing bowl with vinegar and tempeh starter and mix really well. I put on a mask during this step
Step 4 — Fill the bags Put lentils into the bags using the kitchen scale to ensure you’ve got about the same amount in each (usually about 16oz). After you close the bags, gently even them out and press a bit flat.
Step 5 — Set up your incubator Place on a rack in the oven and turn on the oven light but use other pots to keep your tempeh out of direct oven light because otherwise it will get toooo hot. This will act as your incubator.
Step 6 — Ferment for 2 days Let it grow for 2 days! For the first day leave it alone but after that you will see growth (I took a reference picture, see the moisture and “mold” forming) and this is when you monitor the temp w a thermometer, don’t let it go above 95F for prolonged periods. In the last 18 hours or so, you can take it out of the oven esp if it’s warm at 95F and what I like to do is run a desk fan at low setting and let it air and cool and this I’ve seen to bring it down to 88-92F.
Step 7 — Cook and enjoy Once the tempeh is thick and ready like in the pictures, take it out of the plastic, cut it up, pan sear, splash some soy sauce near the end and thats all you need to enjoy this tasty juicy lentil tempeh.
title: Lentil Tempeh

contents: Ingredients 1.5 cups all-purpose flour 1/2 cup granulated sugar 1 tsp baking powder 1 tsp baking soda 1/4 tsp salt 1/2 cup unsweetened plant milk 1/4 cup lemon juice 1/2 cup olive oil 1 tsp vanilla extract 1 cup fresh strawberries, chopped Instructions Preheat oven to 375°F / 190°C Mix dry ingredients in a large bowl Mix wet ingredients separately then fold into dry Fold in strawberries Fill muffin tin 3/4 full Bake 20-25 minutes until golden
title: Strawberry Love Muffins
